Flavor Text: You've made rope! Rope may prove useful while exploring.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Rope item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT29). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a piton! This sturdy spike with a rope attached could be useful for navigating underground tunnels. If you need to make more, you could probably also make one with a sharpened shell.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Piton item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT76). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a snare! You can set this at night, with a bit of bait, and hopefully catch a small animal.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Snare item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT78). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a travois! It's a simple sled that can be dragged behind you, allowing you to haul materials.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Travois item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT27). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a sewing kit! You'll be able to mend clothes with this.

Flavor Text: You've made a bow drill! This advanced tool spins a drill by moving a bow back and forth. It can be used to easily drill holes, or even start fires with friction.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Bow Drill item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT77). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a necklace of bones! This necklace displays your trophies and wearing it makes you feel more capable.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Necklace of Bones item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT46). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made bolas! This thrown weapon is exceptional at catching prey while hunting.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Bolas item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT15). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You carefully tie a string to a piece of the blooming coral and fashion a necklace. You feel strangely invigorated wearing a necklace made from such rare material.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Necklace of Coral item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT47). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Piton item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT76). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a woven basket! This will help you carry some extra material and gather additional nuts or grains while foraging.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Woven Basket item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT28). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Travois item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT27). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a spear from the tooth of a predator! The tooth tip is exceptionally hard and sharp. This spear will be useful for hunting, fishing, and fighting.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Toothed Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT72). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a wooden spear! It's simple, but it will be useful for hunting, fishing, and defending yourself.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Wooden Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT03). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: Two pieces of wood end to end don't create anything useful, perhaps if you rotated one, or if you hardened one of the pieces over a fire to make it stronger…
Game Text: You may attempt to craft using these two materials again or return these materials to your inventory.

Flavor Text: You've made a bone spear! A bone spear is useful for hunting and defending yourself, but especially good for fishing.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Bone Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT04). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a mallet! It can be used to strike and break materials, and also used as a hammer to construct buildings and drive tent stakes. It can also be used as a weapon if necessary.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Mallet item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT21). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made an atlatl! By placing a spear on the atlatl and holding the atlatl, you gain tremendous leverage to throw the spear further and harder.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Atlatl item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT79). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a riverstone spear! A spear is useful for hunting, fishing, and defending yourself.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Riverstone Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT05). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a riverstone adze! An adze is used for digging, and can be used as a decent weapon if necessary.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Riverstone Adze item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT13). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made an atlatl! By placing a spear on the atlatl and holding the atlatl, you gain tremendous leverage to throw the spear further and harder.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Atlatl item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT79). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: While this shape would create a spear, rounded cloudstone isn't a suitable tip. You'll need something sharper. Though, cloudstone could make for a powerful blunt weapon or striking tool...

Flavor Text: You've made a spear from the shell of a predator! This spear is extremely hard and sharp, and useful for hunting, fishing, and defending yourself.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Shell Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT67). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a splint! A splint can be used to help bones mend.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Splint item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT26). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: After pouring some of the liquid moonblood over a flat surface so that it hardens into a thin point under the light of a full moon, you affix an appropriate shaft. The resulting spear is perfectly balanced and deadly sharp. You notice that after each full moon, the point seems even harder and sharper for a short time.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Moonblood Spear item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT58). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You've made a sunstone mallet! It can be used to strike and break materials, and also used as a hammer to construct buildings and drive tent stakes. It can also be used as a weapon if necessary.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Sunstone Mallet item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT22). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: Perhaps if you used regular wood as a handle, you could make a tool with the cloudstone. Similarly, perhaps affixing the hardened wood to another piece of regular wood could yield something useful.
Game Text: Return the materials to your inventory.

Flavor Text: Perhaps if you used regular bone or wood as a handle, you could make a tool with the cloudstone. Similarly, perhaps affixing the carved bone to another piece of regular bone or wood could yield something useful.
Game Text: Return the materials to your inventory.

Flavor Text: You've made a set of spark stones! These stones can be struck together to create sparks and light a fire.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Spark Stones item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT16). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You're quite sure that these materials can be made into something useful, perhaps a different orientation will yield a result.

Flavor Text: After shaping a piece of cloudstone, you paint it with a design to represent the clouds. Much more than just a trinket, this item has important meaning for you.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Cloudstone Talisman item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT52). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: Two cloudstones together don't create anything useful. Perhaps a cloudstone and a different stone would yield a more interesting result…
Game Text: Return the materials to your inventory.

Flavor Text: You've made set of grinding stones! A flat piece of cloudstone serves as the saddle stone and shaped piece of sunstone serves as the grinding stone itself. This item can be used to grind and shape various materials. It can also be used finely grind up material to discover what it produces.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Grinding Stones item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT17). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: The woven sheets of silk are so light, they almost float on the air. Suddenly you have an epiphany! You gather hardened pieces of wood and begin assembling something remarkable. You've made a glider! Despite its size, it's incredibly light! And with a running start you can glide in the air for a very long distance.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Glider item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT82). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: The woven sheets of silk are so light, they almost float on the air. Suddenly you have an epiphany! Perhaps you can fly on in the air like birds do! You gather several carved pieces of bone and begin assembling something remarkable. You've made silk wings! You fashion a harness and strap the wings to your back. While you may not be able to fly over long distances, with a good gust of wind you can take off and soar over any obstacle or danger in your path.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Silk Wings item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT83). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You gather several sheets woven from fine strands of silk, and using strong cord made from animal guts you stitch together a set of clothes. The silk itself is light and strong, and imparts these qualities to anthing made with it. You put on the clothes and somehow feel lighter and more nimble! You'll be able to dodge danger and avoid injury more easily.
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Silk Clothes item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT81). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You take a few smaller sheets of the woven silk and begin weaving them into one massive sheet. You've made a huge net! Despite it's size, it's incredibly light, allowing you to throw it a considerable distance. You'll be able to catch many fish and wild game by surprise with this!
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Sky Net item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT84). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You gather a few sheets woven from fine strands of silk, and using braided plant fiber, you stitch together a pack for carrying things. The silk itself is light and strong, and imparts these qualities to anthing made with it. You fill the pack and hoist it over your shoulder. Somehow the contents feel lighter when inside the pack! You'll be able to easily carry more supplies this way!
Game Text: Discard the materials used and gain the Silk Pack item card (search the Item Card deck facedown for IT80). Record the recipe for this item in your tribe's journal.

Flavor Text: You suspect hide could be stretched over hardened wood to create some kind of structure, but the wood will have to point up (or down) so that the structure has more height.
